title: Pan Seared Filipino Vegetable Lumpia
description: A kid-friendly Filipino dish featuring charred and crunchy vegetable lumpia served in a dolsot stone bowl with a squeeze of lime juice, made by pounding ingredients in a mortar and pestle
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium high heat
In a mortar and pestle, pound the garlic until well crushed
Add the shredded cabbage, grated carrots, and chopped green onions to the mortar and pestle and pound until well combined
Lay a spring roll wrapper on a flat surface and place about 1 tablespoon of the vegetable mixture in the center of the wrapper
Brush the edges of the wrapper with a little water and fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling
Fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight lumpia
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling
When the oil is hot, add a few of the lumpia to the skillet and fry until golden brown and crispy
Remove the lumpia from the oil and place them in a dolsot stone bowl
Serve the lumpia hot with a squeeze of lime juice and a sprinkle of salt
